text.title: Ruby on Rails Framework Fundamentals Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for idiomatic Rails framework usage.

rule.statement: Routes MUST use `resources :name` (and `resource` for singular). Custom routes with `get '/path' => 'controller#action'` are limited to genuinely non-CRUD endpoints.
rule.rationale: Resourceful routing maps to HTTP verbs + named helpers (orders_path, edit_order_path); ad-hoc routes lose this convention.

rule.statement: Controllers MUST use `params.require(:resource).permit(:f1, :f2)` for any mass-assignment. `params[:resource]` raw passing is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Strong Parameters block attacker-controlled keys (is_admin, balance) from reaching create/update.

rule.statement: Secrets MUST live in `config/credentials.yml.enc` (encrypted), not ENV variables for production. ENV is acceptable for non-secret config.
rule.rationale: credentials.yml.enc lets secrets ship with code + uses master key; ENV pollutes deploy config and leaks in error pages.

rule.statement: Logic shared across ≥2 models MUST live in `app/models/concerns/<Name>.rb`. Copy-paste of methods across models is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Concerns reuse + document the abstraction; duplication drifts.

rule.statement: Model callbacks (`before_save`, `after_create`) MUST be limited to internal invariants (data normalization, derived fields). External side effects (send_email, enqueue_job, publish_event) MUST live in services or jobs.
rule.rationale: Callbacks for side effects run in tests + fixtures + console accidentally; service-layer is the proper home.
